seoTitle: How to Add HTML Code to a Canva Website Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to embed custom HTML code into your Canva website to add forms, videos, and buttons without heavy coding knowledge.
intro: Adding custom HTML to a Canva website opens up functionality beyond the built-in design tools, letting you embed forms, videos, or interactive buttons. This guide walks through the process step by step, along with tips for testing and troubleshooting your embedded code.

steps.heading: Understand HTML integration in Canva
steps.body: Get an overview of what adding custom HTML enables on your Canva website before diving into the setup.

steps.heading: Follow the step-by-step instructions
steps.body: Insert your HTML code into the appropriate section of your Canva website editor.

steps.heading: Explore major use cases
steps.body: Consider common applications like embedded forms for lead generation or AI-powered content that responds to visitor preferences.

steps.heading: Test the changes for effectiveness
steps.body: Preview your website after adding the HTML to confirm everything displays and functions as expected.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common errors
steps.body: If something doesn't work as intended, review your code and Canva's HTML embedding requirements to find the issue.

faq.question: Do I need to know how to code to add HTML in Canva?
faq.answer: Basic HTML knowledge helps, but the tutorial walks through the process step by step for users without heavy coding experience.

faq.question: What can I embed using custom HTML in Canva?
faq.answer: Common uses include forms, videos, and buttons that add interactivity beyond Canva's default website tools.

faq.question: Can this help with lead generation for my business?
faq.answer: Yes, embedding forms via HTML is a common way businesses gather leads directly from their Canva website.

faq.question: What should I do if my embedded HTML isn't working?
faq.answer: Check your code for errors and confirm it follows Canva's requirements for embedding custom HTML.
